jokes.text: I get to have sex with the star of the play! -People can hear you. -I know!
jokes.setup: Chandler boasts loudly about sleeping with the star
jokes.punchline: When told people can hear, he responds 'I know!' - he WANTS them to hear
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - his insecurity manifests as overcompensating braggadocio. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Is it just me, or can you actually see his abs through his overcoat?
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on the male actor's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of seeing abs through heavy clothing
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor with visual absurdity, but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: So you been doing this long? No. You're my first. Put the money on the table.
jokes.setup: Sounds like a conversation about prostitution
jokes.punchline: Actually about some other service transaction
jokes.explanation: Generic misdirection joke relying on innuendo without much craft.

jokes.text: -Is that an expensive blouse? -If you want it to be.
jokes.setup: Question about the cost of clothing
jokes.punchline: Suggestive response implying flexible pricing based on desire
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with economy, but lacks character specificity.

jokes.text: Here's your girlfriend's button.
jokes.setup: Someone's girlfriend lost clothing during the play
jokes.punchline: The casual delivery of evidence of partial undressing
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with cringe potential, but needs more context to fully land.

jokes.text: ...throw your purses at it.
jokes.setup: Monica organizing Rachel's belongings
jokes.punchline: Casual instruction to literally throw expensive purses at a pile
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's efficiency extends to casual treatment of luxury items.

jokes.text: What is that? Where you walk around with rich people and tell them what to buy? That sounds great!
jokes.setup: Personal shopping described dismissively
jokes.punchline: Phoebe thinks it sounds amazing
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - what Rachel sees as demeaning, Phoebe finds appealing.

jokes.text: We switched apartments. You can't eat our food. That gravy train has ended. There's gravy?
jokes.setup: Monica uses metaphor about gravy train ending
jokes.punchline: Joey literally thinks there's gravy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his food obsession makes him miss metaphors. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: -What are you doing? -I think I left a doughnut up here.
jokes.setup: Joey doing something unusual in the apartment
jokes.punchline: He's searching for a doughnut in an unexpected location
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey food behavior - the idea he might hide donuts everywhere is character-perfect.

jokes.text: It's like somebody wrote down my worst nightmare... ...and then charged me $32 to see it.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing watching girlfriend simulate sex on stage
jokes.punchline: Adding insult to injury by having to pay for the torture
jokes.explanation: Strong observational humor with good escalation - the money aspect makes the situation more absurd.

jokes.text: That's a good idea for a business.
jokes.setup: Chandler's complaint about paying to see his nightmare
jokes.punchline: Joey sees it as a legitimate business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that shows Joey's unique perspective on situations.

jokes.text: He'll be like, 'Where's your boyfriend? What's his name? Chester?' She'll go, 'It's Chandler.' He'll go, 'Whatever!'
jokes.setup: Chandler imagining how the actor will undermine him
jokes.punchline: The actor dismissively calling him Chester and saying 'Whatever!'
jokes.explanation: Good character neurosis - Chandler's insecurity creates elaborate scenarios of disrespect.

jokes.text: That is a good trick.
jokes.setup: Chandler's paranoid scenario about being undermined
jokes.punchline: Ross treating psychological manipulation as just a 'good trick'
jokes.explanation: Decent reaction beat with Ross's obliviousness to the emotional stakes.

jokes.text: Mel Gibson and Clint Eastwood. They're not a couple. I get the game now.
jokes.setup: Listing celebrity couples who met on movie sets
jokes.punchline: Joey lists two male action stars as if they were a couple
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - the setup makes you expect another real couple, then Joey's confusion creates absurdity.

jokes.text: When they were doing it on stage, was it really hot? Oh, yeah. So you're fine.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if the stage sex was hot
jokes.punchline: Chandler confirms it was, leading to Joey's illogical conclusion he's fine
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Joey's 'help' makes things worse by confirming Chandler's fears.

jokes.text: You guys have been to every play I've been in. Have I ever had chemistry on stage? -No. -No!
jokes.setup: Joey asks about his stage chemistry
jokes.punchline: His friends immediately and emphatically confirm he has none
jokes.explanation: Good setup with immediate, devastating honesty from his friends. Joey's acting limitations as comedy.

jokes.text: Getting closer. Don't worry... we also have cereals, muffins, waffles and... jams, jellies and marmalades, which I'm sure are the same thing.
jokes.setup: Monica desperately trying to get someone to eat
jokes.punchline: Her manic listing and admission she doesn't know the difference between preserves
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's need to host even when desperate. The preserve confusion adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Please eat, because Joey opened everything.
jokes.setup: Monica's desperation to get people to eat her food
jokes.punchline: Joey opened all the food packages, creating waste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey callback - his food enthusiasm creates problems. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah? Who's playing? The players.
jokes.setup: Ross asks who's playing in the game
jokes.punchline: Joey's uselessly obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey non-answer that shows his sports knowledge limitations.

jokes.text: Please. It's a relief, is what it is, is what it is.
jokes.setup: Monica claiming she doesn't miss being the hostess
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive, unconvincing delivery reveals she's lying to herself
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Monica's repetition and emphasis betray her true feelings. The stammering feels authentic.

jokes.text: I just helped an 81-year-old woman put on a thong, and she didn't even buy it.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about her personal shopping job
jokes.punchline: The disturbing mental image of elderly woman trying on intimate apparel
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe comedy with vivid, uncomfortable imagery. The 'didn't buy it' kicker adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: Anyway, she burned all of my clothes. I got away with this suit and what turned out to be a skirt.
jokes.setup: Joshua explaining his ex-wife burned his clothes
jokes.punchline: He escaped with a suit and accidentally a skirt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection - the 'what turned out to be' construction works well.

jokes.text: It wasn't much fun dropping it off at the dry cleaners in the skirt.
jokes.setup: Following up on the skirt revelation
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing practical consequence of wearing the skirt
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the skirt gag, extending the comedy with practical implications.

jokes.text: You needed to speak to me? No, no, that wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Boss asking if Rachel needed to speak to him
jokes.punchline: Rachel denying it because Joshua walked in
jokes.explanation: Decent situational comedy with Rachel's quick reversal when distracted by attraction.

jokes.text: Let me show you my underwear. The selection of underwear that we carry.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering to show Joshua underwear
jokes.punchline: Quick clarification that she means the store's selection
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre with quick recovery. Rachel's attraction making her flustered.

jokes.text: Come on. It was like cousins having sex.
jokes.setup: Ross describing the lack of chemistry in the play
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the most unsexy scenario possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant simile with maximum economy - captures unsexy chemistry perfectly with disturbing comparison.

jokes.text: Don't say a word. Just be cool. Don't be, you know, you.
jokes.setup: Ross giving Chandler advice on staying calm
jokes.punchline: Telling him not to be himself
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Ross knows Chandler's neurotic nature will make things worse. Good economy.

jokes.text: Clearly, you're having sex with him.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to be cool and subtle
jokes.punchline: Immediately blurting out the accusation bluntly
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Ross's warning - Chandler immediately does exactly what he shouldn't. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: You're accusing me of cheating on you, and you're insulting my performance?
jokes.setup: Chandler's accusation based on lack of stage chemistry
jokes.punchline: Kathy realizing he's simultaneously calling her unfaithful AND a bad actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent observation of the double insult inherent in Chandler's logic. Shows how his 'evidence' backfires.

jokes.text: I have the best job in the entire world! The most adorable guy came over, and I got to dress him up all day!
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about her work day
jokes.punchline: Her describing a professional interaction like playing with dolls
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's attraction making her see work as play. The doll metaphor works.

jokes.text: Rachel has a new doll!
jokes.setup: Rachel's description of dressing up Joshua
jokes.punchline: Monica literally calling him Rachel's new doll
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the subtext text. Monica's directness works for the character.

jokes.text: I wish he was a doll. I'd get a Rachel doll and bump them together... ...and make kissing noises.
jokes.setup: Rachel wishing Joshua was actually a doll
jokes.punchline: Her describing elaborate doll play fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the doll metaphor into childish fantasy. Shows depth of Rachel's crush.

jokes.text: He's got the most beautiful name. I never realized. Joshua. Joshua. Josh.
jokes.setup: Rachel praising Joshua's name
jokes.punchline: Her obsessive repetition of a completely ordinary name
jokes.explanation: Great portrayal of infatuation - how attraction makes ordinary things seem special. The repetition sells it.

jokes.text: Cookies and porn? You're the best mom ever!
jokes.setup: Monica providing snacks and magazines
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate motherly comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - Joey's innocent appreciation of hospitality in inappropriate terms. Great economy.

jokes.text: How does Jason look in a V-neck? -It's Joshua. -Whatever.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about Joshua
jokes.punchline: She calls him Jason, dismisses the correction with 'Whatever'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Phoebe's casual indifference to names Rachel finds beautiful. Contrasts their perspectives.

jokes.text: I served the best air.
jokes.setup: Monica reminiscing about childhood tea parties
jokes.punchline: Proudly remembering serving imaginary refreshments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica line - her perfectionism extends even to imaginary food. Brilliant economy and character insight.

jokes.text: Slut!
jokes.setup: Chandler drunkenly stumbling into the apartment
jokes.punchline: His random, slurred insult at no one in particular
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy with drunk Chandler. The randomness works for the state he's in.

jokes.text: I don't live here!
jokes.setup: Chandler continuing drunk behavior
jokes.punchline: His confused realization about apartment location
jokes.explanation: Decent drunk confusion gag. Works with the apartment switch situation.

jokes.text: Then I went over to Beefsteak Chewley's.... -Beefsteak Charlie's? -Yes!
jokes.setup: Chandler mispronouncing the restaurant name while drunk
jokes.punchline: Ross correcting him, Chandler agreeing without realizing the error
jokes.explanation: Good drunk speech pattern with malapropism. Shows Chandler's state while Ross plays straight man.

jokes.text: You and I have always been like:
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to express friendship with gestures
jokes.punchline: Unable to complete the thought, just making meaningless gestures
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing drunk inability to articulate emotions. The incomplete gesture is relatable.

jokes.text: I don't remember it.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming the bartender taught him a trick
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission he doesn't remember it
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic and economy. The immediate contradiction is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That's such a turn-on. Really? It doesn't seem desperate? That's the turn-on.
jokes.setup: Rachel worried about seeming desperate by asking men out
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claiming desperation itself is attractive
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal of conventional dating wisdom. Phoebe's unconventional logic works for her character.

jokes.text: You've never asked a guy out? No. Have you? Thousands of times!
jokes.setup: Joey surprised Rachel never asked men out
jokes.punchline: His casual admission to doing it 'thousands of times'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's reversed gender dynamics. The 'thousands' is typical Joey exaggeration.

jokes.text: That doesn't make me sound too good, does it?
jokes.setup: Joey's boast about asking out thousands of people
jokes.punchline: His delayed realization this might not sound impressive
jokes.explanation: Good Joey self-awareness moment. Shows his occasional insight into how he comes across.

jokes.text: What I do is, I look a woman up and down, and I say... 'Hey. How you doing?'
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his pickup technique
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase delivered as serious dating advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey catchphrase moment. While not original, it's perfectly integrated and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, please! Hey. How you doing?
jokes.setup: Rachel dismissing Joey's technique as ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately trying it on Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with Phoebe's playfulness. Shows the line might actually work.

jokes.text: Well, let's see. This license belongs to... ...belongs to a Mr. Pheebs.
jokes.setup: Rachel pretending to check ID in a found wallet
jokes.punchline: The wallet obviously belongs to Phoebe, who's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Rachel's obvious lie. The 'Mr. Pheebs' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: -You've done that a thousand times? -I've never done that.
jokes.setup: Rachel referencing Phoebe's earlier claim about asking men out
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admitting she's never actually done it
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing Phoebe was giving advice she's never tested. Classic Phoebe contradiction.

jokes.text: There's a lot of theories that didn't pan out: Lone gunman, communism, geometry.
jokes.setup: Joey defending his relationship theory being wrong
jokes.punchline: Listing failed theories including basic mathematics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey moment - equating conspiracy theories with math shows his unique worldview. Perfect escalation and economy.

jokes.text: 'Who likes the Knick?'
jokes.setup: Rachel practicing asking Joshua to the game
jokes.punchline: Her grammatically incorrect, awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good nervous Rachel moment. The grammar mistake shows how flustered she is about asking him out.

jokes.text: That would be fantastic! My nephew is crazy about the Knicks!
jokes.setup: Rachel finally asking Joshua to the game
jokes.punchline: He accepts but wants to bring his nephew, missing the romantic intent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how Rachel's indirect approach backfired. Classic dating miscommunication.

jokes.text: Nick's pants?
jokes.setup: Chandler finding men's pants at Kathy's place
jokes.punchline: The visual evidence confirming his worst fears
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag with perfect economy. Two words that confirm everything Chandler feared.

jokes.text: He didn't turn me down. He's at the game. I got the date. I'm just not on it.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her 'successful' ask
jokes.punchline: Technically got a yes but completely failed at the actual goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent Rachel rationalization showing her putting positive spin on complete failure. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: What do you think of the floor? I don't know. Looks the same. You used to have carpet.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about floor after working two days
jokes.punchline: Joey's oblivious response to major renovation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's obliviousness to Monica's efforts. The understatement works well.

jokes.text: I'm always the hostess.
jokes.setup: Monica falling asleep while trying to host
jokes.punchline: Her sleepy mumble about her identity as hostess
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica callback - even unconscious, she's thinking about hosting. Shows how core it is to her identity.

jokes.text: Nick and his rock-hard pecs and his giant man-nipples.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing Nick's physical attributes
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific detail about 'giant man-nipples'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with increasingly absurd physical details. 'Giant man-nipples' is memorably specific.

jokes.text: They wouldn't have ended up in bed. I threw her at his man-nipples!
jokes.setup: Chandler blaming himself for Kathy cheating
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of literally throwing her at Nick's nipples
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with escalation. The visual of 'throwing her at his man-nipples' is absurdly memorable.

jokes.text: Well, if she thought they were on a break....
jokes.setup: Ross trying to offer perspective on Chandler's situation
jokes.punchline: He trails off realizing he's referencing his own relationship disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to show Ross's self-awareness. His trailing off shows he realizes the parallel.

jokes.text: I left my wallet here on purpose. Really? Yes. I just wanted to see you again.
jokes.setup: The wallet return seeming like coincidence
jokes.punchline: Joshua revealing it was intentional to see Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the wallet setup. Shows Joshua was interested all along, reversing the dynamic.

jokes.text: 'How you doing?'
jokes.setup: Joshua saying he wants to tell Rachel something
jokes.punchline: He uses Joey's signature pickup line
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Joey's line actually working. Validates his technique and gives Rachel success.
